  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I (A) do light copy-editing only—sentence/paragraph-level grammar, clarity, and concision while preserving structure and claims—or (B) also perform higher-level rewriting that reframes the contribution/gap and reorders the abstract’s narrative, potentially tightening or hedging claims? Please reply with A or B.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The phrase 'Clean this up' most directly maps to improving sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, grammar, and concision for the abstract.",
    "RX.1": "The scope is ambiguous between copy-editing and deeper reframing, so the uncertainty must be surfaced before proceeding.",
    "RX.2": "Edits should avoid inadvertently amplifying novelty or proof-strength claims beyond what the text currently supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work must stay within the user-selected scope and avoid altering technical content unless explicitly requested.",
    "RX.4": "Misrepresenting formal guarantees could mislead readers or reviewers, so edits should not introduce harmful inaccuracies.",
    "RX.5": "Any changes should be reviewable and attributable to preserve provenance and allow traceability of edits."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should I (A) do light copy-editing only—sentence/paragraph-level grammar, clarity, and concision while preserving structure and claims—or (B) also perform higher-level rewriting that reframes the contribution/gap and reorders the abstract’s narrative, potentially tightening or hedging claims? Please reply with A or B.

rationale.R1.6: The phrase 'Clean this up' most directly maps to improving sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, grammar, and concision for the abstract.
rationale.RX.1: The scope is ambiguous between copy-editing and deeper reframing, so the uncertainty must be surfaced before proceeding.
rationale.RX.2: Edits should avoid inadvertently amplifying novelty or proof-strength claims beyond what the text currently supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work must stay within the user-selected scope and avoid altering technical content unless explicitly requested.
rationale.RX.4: Misrepresenting formal guarantees could mislead readers or reviewers, so edits should not introduce harmful inaccuracies.
rationale.RX.5: Any changes should be reviewable and attributable to preserve provenance and allow traceability of edits.
